draft.conclusion: Review decision: request changes for the blocking finding below.

findings.problem: The patch blocks only protocol-relative URLs and still permits http:// or https:// absolute URLs. https:// 绝对 absolute baseurl http 绕过 绝对 覆盖 absolute authorization scheme bypass
findings.evidence: http://evil.test and https://evil.test are also absolute URLs that make Axios ignore baseURL, so a caller-controlled absolute URL sends the internal Authorization header to an attacker host (credential leak) or an internal address (SSRF). caller-controlled authorization
findings.impact: http://evil.test and https://evil.test are also absolute URLs that make Axios ignore baseURL, so a caller-controlled absolute URL sends the internal Authorization header to an attacker host (credential leak) or an internal address (SSRF). caller-controlled authorization
findings.fix: Parse the final URL and reject any origin outside the configured baseURL, or disable absolute URLs for this client (e.g. allowAbsoluteUrls: false). allowabsoluteurls configured

draft.tests: Tests cover //evil.test but not https://evil.test or http://evil.test. The vulnerable absolute-URL path is still untested, and nothing asserts the Authorization header is not sent off-host. 测试 https://evil test absolute 测试 绝对 test https 测试 authorization
